section.heading: Conduct involving trade or commerce with foreign nations
section.chapeau: Sections 1 to 7 of this title shall not apply to conduct involving trade or commerce (other than import trade or import commerce) with foreign nations unless—

paragraph.chapeau: such conduct has a direct, substantial, and reasonably foreseeable effect—

subparagraph.content: on trade or commerce which is not trade or commerce with foreign nations, or on import trade or import commerce with foreign nations; or
on export trade or export commerce with foreign nations, of a person engaged in such trade or commerce in the United States; and

paragraph.content: such effect gives rise to a claim under the provisions of sections 1 to 7 of this title, other than this section.

section.continuation: If sections 1 to 7 of this title apply to such conduct only because of the operation of paragraph (1)(B), then sections 1 to 7 of this title shall apply to such conduct only for injury to export business in the United States.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 7 of act July 2, 1890, ch. 647, 26 Stat. 210, related to suits by persons injured by acts in violation of sections 1 to 7 of this title and was classified as a note under section 15 of this title, prior to repeal by act July 7, 1955, ch. 283, § 3, 69 Stat. 283, effective six months after July 7, 1955.
